interpretation: The curve is broadly upward-sloping but exhibits a notable hump: the 2Y yield (11.95%) exceeds the 5Y (10.82%) and 10Y (11.15%), while the 364D dips below 182D, creating short-end distortion. This reflects tight banking sector liquidity driving up near-term borrowing costs, with BB's repo rate at 10% anchoring the front end. Elevated inflation expectations and fiscal borrowing pressure push the long end higher. The 2Y bulge suggests banks are demanding a premium for medium-term government paper amid uncertain monetary policy trajectory.
implications: Tight BDT liquidity and aggressive BB tightening are pressuring the 1-2Y segment, signaling banks prefer ultra-short or long-duration over medium tenors
The 130bps 10Y-91D spread with a humped mid-section suggests market expectations that BB may hold rates high near-term before eventual easing, creating inversion risk at the belly
Persistent double-digit yields across the curve indicate elevated inflation expectations and heavy government domestic borrowing, crowding out private sector credit growth
